evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight review skill. A pre-scaffolded project exists at /Users/nic/src/ai-home/preflight/.worktrees/feature-reviewer-agents/tests/fixtures/scaffolded-project/. Use that directory as your working directory for all file operations.

Run /preflight review docs/decisions/adrs/adr-001-use-markdown-templates.md

This is a well-formed ADR with proper MADR structure, frontmatter, all required sections, and valid cross-references. The review should find no Critical findings. Minor suggestions are acceptable.

Save the full review output to outputs/review-adr-good.txt
evals.expected_output: Review runs successfully. Dispatches both checklist-reviewer and bogey-reviewer agents. Loads universal-rules.md and adr-rules.md. Result has 0 Critical findings. May have suggestions. Report format matches SKILL.md section 9. Findings cite rule IDs or 'structural' source.

assertions.text: Both reviewer agents (checklist and bogey) were dispatched
adr-rules.md and universal-rules.md were loaded (not requirements-rules or other wrong type)
Zero Critical findings reported for this well-formed ADR
Report format matches SKILL.md section 9 (severity groups, source attribution, summary line)
Document was NOT modified by the review

evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight review skill. A pre-scaffolded project exists at /Users/nic/src/ai-home/preflight/.worktrees/feature-reviewer-agents/tests/fixtures/scaffolded-project/. Use that directory as your working directory for all file operations.

Run /preflight review docs/requirements.md

This requirements document has these intentional violations:
- 2 requirements missing FR-NNN IDs (prose sentences without IDs)
- 2 requirements using 'should' and 'can' instead of 'shall' (non-EARS language)
- 3 NFRs with vague, non-measurable criteria (NFR-001 'fast', NFR-002 'secure', NFR-003 'good uptime')

The review should catch most of these. It must NOT modify the document.

Save the full review output to outputs/review-requirements-bad.txt
evals.expected_output: Review finds multiple violations. Reports findings for: missing IDs, non-EARS language (should/can), vague NFRs. Each finding cites a rule ID. Document not modified. Summary shows counts by severity.

assertions.text: Both reviewer agents (checklist and bogey) were dispatched
cross-doc-rules.md was loaded (doc references FR-NNN and NFR-NNN IDs)
At least one finding about missing requirement IDs
At least one finding about non-EARS language (should/can instead of shall)
At least one finding about vague/non-measurable NFR criteria
Report format matches SKILL.md section 9 with severity grouping and summary line
Document was NOT modified by the review

evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight review skill's initialization guard. Use /tmp/preflight-uninitialized-review/ as your working directory. Create it fresh:

mkdir -p /tmp/preflight-uninitialized-review/docs

Create a minimal doc at docs/requirements.md with content '# Requirements\n\n- FR-001: The system shall exist.'

Do NOT create any .preflight/ directory.

Now run /preflight review docs/requirements.md

The skill should detect that .preflight/_rules/ does not exist and stop with a clear error telling the user to run /preflight scaffold first.

Save the skill's response to outputs/guard-output.txt
evals.expected_output: Skill stops at section 4.0 guard. Message tells user to run /preflight scaffold first. Does NOT attempt to load rules, dispatch agents, or review any document.

assertions.text: Skill stopped before dispatching any reviewer agents
